lead.likelyMechanism: The presence of the blaKPC and blaSHV genes suggests that this isolate may produce carbapenemases, which can hydrolyze meropenem and lead to resistance. Additionally, the aac(6') gene indicates potential aminoglycoside resistance, which may complicate treatment options.
lead.recommendedAction: Perform a phenotypic susceptibility test for meropenem to confirm resistance.
lead.reasoning: Given the presence of multiple resistance determinants, including known carbapenemases, a phenotypic test will provide critical information on the actual susceptibility of this isolate to meropenem.

lead.likelyMechanism: The presence of aac(6') suggests potential aminoglycoside resistance, while blakpc and blashv indicate beta-lactamase production that could hydrolyze ceftriaxone. The emrab determinant may confer resistance to carbapenems, and fosa and oqxab could indicate efflux pump activity, further complicating the resistance profile.
lead.recommendedAction: Perform phenotypic susceptibility testing for ceftriaxone to confirm resistance.
lead.reasoning: Given the complex resistance determinants present, phenotypic testing will provide a clearer picture of the isolate's susceptibility to ceftriaxone, which is critical for effective treatment.

provenance.training: NCBI Pathogen Detection — AMRFinderPlus genotypes (acquired genes, point mutations, truncations)
provenance.external: BV-BRC laboratory-confirmed phenotypes, gene presence only
provenance.note: Predictions precomputed offline so the demo cannot fail live. The pipeline runs for real in the tech video.

note: Clinical content — predictions, evidence, abstentions and lookup comparisons — is computed from held-out isolates. Accessions, wards, requesting clinicians and timestamps are synthetic, to show the workflow a laboratory would use.
